_status:
DRAFT — review artifact, not loaded by dialogue_runner. Per PROPOSAL_player_driven_argument.md §1 (Whimsy subsection), reshapes before_meeting so that Cula opens with a QUESTION rather than the conclusion. The current opening line ('Notice went to an address the client left two years ago. The objection is in the papers; the record does not show it') becomes one of three Cula options — the procedurally well-fitted pitch. Two alternative pitches give the player a chance to mis-frame; Whimsy's response varies by framing, and the choice writes chapter1.whimsy_co_counsel_posture (string enum declared at SAVE_VERSION 17). All three paths still recruit Whimsy; the variance is in what KIND of co-counsel Whimsy becomes — a flag the Phase 2 closing-argument controller will read for flavor lines.

Merge strategy: when ready, take this draft, open the live whimsy.json, replace the body of before_meeting. All other states (after_recruitment_client_upcoming, after_recruitment_court_ready, after_recruitment_idle, the four coffee_reaction states, idle_flavor) remain unchanged.

Voice discipline: Whimsy is grandiose, theatrical, brilliant-when-aimed; sanctioned em-dash latitude (≤1 per line still). Per narrative_revision/voice_agents/whimsy.md.
_design_role_handoff: All three Cula option-texts and three Whimsy-response variants need final voicing. The procedural-throat path preserves the current voice closely; the merits-pivot and open-register paths are new line shapes that should be voiced from scratch against whimsy.md.

states._comment:
RESHAPED — LOAD-BEARING. Cula opens with the QUESTION she wants help with (not the conclusion). Three Cula options correspond to three rhetorical postures Whimsy can be recruited under. The options block writes chapter1.whimsy_co_counsel_posture (string enum, v17). chain:true so Whimsy responds in-state after the pick. The three responses still all recruit (set met_whimsy + recruited_whimsy on on_dismiss). The current 'fair-hearing argument can sing' line is preserved verbatim as Whimsy's response to the procedural_throat path.

Note: the current cula-opens-with-conclusion line ('Notice went to an address the client left two years ago...') is removed from this state. It surfaces, if at all, as Whimsy's restatement back at Cula on the procedural_throat path.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Mr. Whimsy. Cula, Pig & Swine. We need a second voice in court tomorrow.

lines: Cula. Sit. Have you anything beyond procedural vibes, or am I to be recruited on atmosphere?

choices.text: Notice went to the wrong door — landlord knew. I need the fair-hearing rebuttal in your voice.
He cannot evict her — she has held that flat since eighty-seven. Argue the tenancy.
Tell me the geometry of the wrong. I'll know it when I hear it.

states._comment: NEW state. Whimsy's response on the well-fitted procedural pitch. Preserves the current 'fair-hearing argument can sing' voice. The on_dismiss writes met_whimsy + recruited_whimsy.
lines: Ah. The fair-hearing doctrine, asked to do its gymnastics again. Notice to an address without a client; an objection present in paper, absent from the record. I have heard worse music.
Marginalia in espresso. Half the argument is already mine.
I propose. The fair-hearing argument can sing. The service defect gives it a throat. I will lend my voice tomorrow at fourteen hundred.

states._comment: NEW state. Whimsy's response on the wrong-shape pitch — he walks Cula back to the procedural posture. Recruits anyway: Whimsy will lend rhetoric to whatever Cula carries into court. The flavor on the Phase 2 closing arguments will read differently depending on this posture flag.
lines: The merits — at the motion stage. You wish to argue, before the same court that has already heard the landlord uncontested, that the tenancy is valid.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: She has held it since eighty-seven.

lines: She has. And the court will hear that when it hears the case. The motion stage is a door, not a hearing. We are arguing that the door was closed wrongly.
I will follow you in. I am cheaper than the door.

states._comment: NEW state. Whimsy's response when Cula asks for help without having committed to a shape. Whimsy probes; Cula has not handed him a frame, so he asks for one. This is the path the player takes when she wants the chapter to slow down. Recruits.
lines: The geometry. You walk in with a question rather than a verdict.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: I walked in with a binder and three pages of fact.

lines: And no shape yet. Acceptable. The shape will arrive when Crab finishes counting his envelopes.
I propose this. You return with a frame. I lend my voice to whatever frame you bring. The voice is the easy part.

idle_flavor: A procedural argument without a metaphor is like coffee without water. Dry and slightly offensive.
